title: English Articles — Morning Routine
text: I made ___ cup of coffee and read ___ newspaper before going to ___ office.

blanks.explanation: 'The' refers to a specific, known newspaper.
'The' refers to the speaker's specific office.

questions.question: What is the capital of France?
questions.explanation: Paris has been the capital of France since the 10th century.

questions.question: What is the capital of Japan?
questions.explanation: Tokyo has been the capital since 1868.

questions.question: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.explanation: Canberra was purpose-built as the capital as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne.

questions.statement: The Sun revolves around the Earth.
questions.explanation: The Earth revolves around the Sun.

questions.statement: Water boils at 100°C at standard atmospheric pressure.
questions.explanation: At 1 atm, water's boiling point is exactly 100°C.

questions.statement: Sound travels faster in water than in air.
questions.explanation: Sound travels about 4x faster in water (~1,480 m/s) than in air (~343 m/s).

title: English Articles — At the Zoo
text: ___ elephant was eating ___ banana near ___ tree.

questions.question: What is 15% of 200?
Which number is prime?
questions.explanation: 29 is only divisible by 1 and itself.

questions.question: What is the square root of 144?
Solve: 3x + 7 = 22. What is x?
Which word is closest in meaning to 'benevolent'?
questions.explanation: 'Benevolent' means well-meaning and kindly.

questions.question: Which word is closest in meaning to 'ephemeral'?
questions.explanation: 'Ephemeral' means lasting for a very short time.

questions.question: Which word is closest in meaning to 'ubiquitous'?
questions.explanation: 'Ubiquitous' means present, appearing, or found everywhere.

questions.statement: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.
questions.explanation: This is a common myth. The wall is too narrow to be seen from space without aid.

questions.statement: World War I began in 1914.
questions.explanation: WWI started on July 28, 1914.

questions.statement: The ancient Romans used concrete in their buildings.
questions.explanation: Roman concrete (opus caementicium) was used extensively, including in the Pantheon.

questions.statement: Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the building of the Great Pyramid.
questions.explanation: The Great Pyramid was built ~2560 BC, Cleopatra lived ~30 BC, and the Moon landing was 1969 AD.

title: English Articles — Weekend Hike
text: Last Saturday, Alex decided to climb ___ mountain near the town. He packed ___ backpack with food and started early in ___ morning. After a few hours, he reached ___ top and enjoyed ___ breathtaking view.

blanks.explanation: 'A' introduces a nonspecific mountain mentioned for the first time.
'The' identifies the specific top of that mountain.
'A' introduces the view as one example among many.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile is approximately 6,650 km long.

questions.question: Which continent has the most countries?
questions.explanation: Africa has 54 recognized countries.

questions.question: What is the smallest country in the world by area?
questions.explanation: Vatican City is only about 0.44 km².

questions.question: What is the key difference between generative and discriminative models?
options: Generative models classify data, discriminative models generate new data.
Generative models create new content, discriminative models classify existing data.
Discriminative models use transformers, generative models do not.
questions.explanation: Generative models create new outputs; discriminative models classify or analyze existing data.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a core output type of generative AI?
questions.explanation: Spreadsheet sorting is a traditional deterministic task, not generative AI output.

questions.statement: The transformer architecture was introduced in the 2017 paper 'Attention Is All You Need.'
questions.explanation: The transformer architecture was introduced by Google researchers in 2017.

questions.statement: Transformers process tokens strictly sequentially like RNNs.
questions.explanation: Transformers process tokens in parallel using self-attention.

questions.statement: Next-token prediction is the core training objective of LLMs.
questions.explanation: LLMs learn by predicting the next token in a sequence.

questions.question: What does a 768-dimensional embedding represent?
options: A token represented by 768 numerical features
questions.explanation: Each embedding dimension is a numeric feature encoding semantic properties.

questions.question: Why are similar words close in embedding space?
options: They are manually programmed that way.
They appear in similar contexts during training.
questions.explanation: Embeddings are learned from contextual similarity during training.

title: Inside a Transformer Layer
text: Each transformer layer contains ___ and ___ as its two primary components.

blanks.explanation: Attention allows the model to weigh relationships between tokens.
The feed-forward network applies non-linear transformations.

questions.statement: Different attention heads can specialize in syntax, semantics, or long-range dependencies.
questions.explanation: Multiple heads learn different relational patterns.

questions.statement: Attention patterns are explicitly programmed by engineers.
questions.explanation: They emerge from training data.

questions.question: Modern LLMs are typically trained on how many tokens?
questions.explanation: Frontier models train on trillions of tokens.

questions.question: What is RLHF primarily used for?
questions.explanation: RLHF teaches models to produce responses humans prefer.

questions.statement: Larger context windows allow models to analyze entire codebases or long documents.
questions.explanation: More tokens in context enable broader analysis.

questions.statement: Longer contexts always improve accuracy.
questions.explanation: Very long contexts may reduce focus and accuracy.

questions.question: What does SWE-bench Verified measure?
questions.explanation: It measures practical software engineering performance.

questions.question: Why are benchmark scores not enough for model selection?
options: They don't reflect real-world use cases.
questions.explanation: Real-world performance must be validated on your own use case.

questions.question: Approximately what percentage of global AI compute capacity is controlled by the US?
questions.explanation: The US controls about 74% of global AI compute capacity.

questions.question: What is one effect of US semiconductor export controls?
options: Restricted access to advanced chips in China
questions.explanation: Export controls limit access to advanced chips like H100.

questions.statement: API integration is typically faster to deploy than local model hosting.
questions.explanation: APIs allow deployment within minutes or hours.

questions.statement: Local deployment eliminates the need for ML expertise.
questions.explanation: Local deployment requires specialized infrastructure and ML operations skills.

questions.question: What is the purpose of few-shot examples in prompts?
questions.explanation: Few-shot examples help models learn the expected output pattern.

questions.question: What does temperature control?
questions.explanation: Lower temperature = more deterministic; higher = more creative.

title: Monitoring AI Systems
text: Key production metrics include P95 ___, error rate, and cost per ___.

blanks.explanation: P95 latency tracks response time performance.
Cost per successful resolution measures ROI.

questions.question: What is prompt caching primarily used for?
options: Reducing cost on repeated prompt prefixes
questions.explanation: Caching reused prompt sections reduces token cost.

questions.question: Why use smaller models for simple tasks?
options: They reduce average cost per query.
questions.explanation: Right-sizing models reduces cost while maintaining adequate performance.

questions.statement: Hallucination rates in frontier models typically range between 2–5% on factual queries.
questions.explanation: Frontier models generally fall within that range.

questions.statement: Prompt injection attacks attempt to override system instructions.
questions.explanation: They exploit model instruction hierarchy.

questions.question: Which capability is considered emergent at scale?
questions.explanation: Advanced reasoning emerges in large-scale models.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)?
options: To retrieve external knowledge and ground model responses
questions.explanation: RAG retrieves relevant documents from external sources and injects them into the prompt to improve factual grounding.

questions.question: What component is typically required in a RAG system?
questions.explanation: RAG systems commonly use a vector database to retrieve semantically similar documents.

questions.statement: Fine-tuning modifies model weights, while prompt engineering does not.
questions.explanation: Fine-tuning updates parameters; prompt engineering changes only the input instructions.

questions.statement: Fine-tuning is always cheaper and faster than prompt engineering.
questions.explanation: Fine-tuning requires training compute and data preparation, making it more expensive and complex.

questions.question: Why are vector databases used in AI applications?
options: To perform similarity search over embeddings
questions.explanation: Vector databases enable efficient nearest-neighbor search in embedding space.

questions.question: Which metric is commonly used to measure embedding similarity?
questions.explanation: Cosine similarity measures the angle between vectors, indicating semantic similarity.

questions.question: What is quantization in the context of LLMs?
options: Reducing numerical precision to make models smaller and faster
questions.explanation: Quantization reduces precision (e.g., FP16 → INT8) to improve efficiency.

questions.question: What is model distillation?
options: Training a smaller model to mimic a larger one
questions.explanation: Distillation transfers knowledge from a large teacher model to a smaller student model.

title: Evaluation Strategies
text: Offline evaluation uses static test datasets, while ___ evaluation measures model performance in real-world production environments.

blanks.explanation: Online evaluation measures live system performance using real user interactions.

questions.statement: Tool-using agents can call APIs, run code, or access databases during reasoning.
questions.explanation: Modern agents integrate external tools to extend capability.

questions.statement: Agentic systems eliminate the need for monitoring and guardrails.
questions.explanation: Agents increase complexity and require stronger monitoring and safety controls.

questions.question: Which factor most directly increases inference latency?
questions.explanation: Larger models require more computation per token.

questions.question: Horizontal scaling in AI systems typically means:
options: Adding more model replicas to handle traffic
questions.explanation: Horizontal scaling distributes load across multiple instances.

questions.question: What is data minimization in AI systems?
options: Collecting and processing only necessary data
questions.explanation: Data minimization reduces privacy risk by limiting collected information.

questions.question: Why maintain audit logs in AI systems?
questions.explanation: Audit logs enable accountability and regulatory compliance.

questions.statement: Model drift occurs when real-world data changes over time, reducing model performance.
questions.explanation: Distribution shifts can degrade performance.

questions.statement: LLMs deployed via API are immune to drift.
questions.explanation: Upstream model updates or usage changes can introduce behavioral drift.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of open-weight models?
options: Full control over deployment and fine-tuning
questions.explanation: Open models allow customization and on-prem deployment.

questions.question: What is a common trade-off with closed proprietary models?
questions.explanation: Closed models may limit visibility into training data and create vendor lock-in.

questions.question: If two pieces of the same metal touch in space, what happens?
options: They bounce apart due to vacuum pressure.
They can permanently bond together (cold welding).
questions.explanation: In a vacuum, without oxidation layers, clean metal surfaces can fuse together through cold welding.

questions.statement: Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.
questions.explanation: Octopuses have three hearts, and their blood is blue because it uses copper-based hemocyanin instead of iron-based hemoglobin.

questions.statement: Sharks are older than trees.
questions.explanation: Sharks have existed for over 400 million years, while the first trees appeared around 350 million years ago.

questions.question: What percentage of your body’s atoms are replaced every year?
questions.explanation: Through cellular turnover and metabolic processes, most atoms in your body are replaced each year.

questions.question: Bananas are technically classified as what?
questions.explanation: Botanically speaking, bananas qualify as berries, while strawberries do not.

title: Sleeping
text: How many tiems has Martin failed to wake up for daily stand-up this year?

blanks.explanation: At least acording to my memorry.

questions.statement: This marks the beginning of the eKasa presentation question set.
questions.explanation: Separator marker — questions 41 through 67 cover the eKasa presentation slides.

questions.question: What does the 'e' in eKasa stand for?
questions.explanation: 'e' stands for 'elektronická' — the Slovak word for electronic.

questions.question: Which Slovak authority operates the eKasa system?
questions.explanation: FRSR — the Slovak Financial Directorate — runs eKasa. It's Slovakia's equivalent of the IRS / HMRC.

questions.question: In which year did eKasa go live?
questions.explanation: eKasa launched in 2019 and has been evolving legally ever since.

questions.statement: eKasa requires every receipt to be sent to the tax authority's servers within seconds of being issued.
questions.explanation: That real-time element is the core of the system — older ERP systems only stored data locally.

questions.statement: Data messages between certified registers and eKasa are sent as JSON over plain HTTP.
questions.explanation: Messages are XML, transported over HTTPS, and digitally signed.

questions.question: Which law currently governs revenue recording (eKasa) in Slovakia?
questions.explanation: Act 384/2025 Coll. replaced older legislation and added support for software-based registers (SORP).

title: Why eKasa Exists — The VAT Gap

questions.question: Slovakia's VAT gap BEFORE eKasa was approximately…
questions.explanation: About 41% — among the worst in the EU. The EU average was around 10%.

questions.question: After eKasa, Slovakia's VAT gap dropped to roughly…
questions.explanation: 26% — a 15-percentage-point improvement, generating around €3.7 billion in extra state revenue.

questions.question: On which date did eKasa become mandatory for newly established businesses?
questions.explanation: April 1, 2019 for new businesses; July 1, 2019 for all existing businesses.

questions.statement: A taxi driver may bundle a whole day's fares into a single end-of-day receipt.
questions.explanation: Forbidden — every individual fare must be its own receipt.

questions.statement: A taxi driver who receives a tip must record it as a VKLAD (deposit) with its own receipt.
questions.explanation: Tips are recorded as VKLAD entries in eKasa.

questions.statement: Issuing a B2B invoice without any cash payment automatically triggers eKasa.
questions.explanation: The trigger is the payment, not the invoice itself.

questions.question: Which group is exempt from using eKasa?
options: Severely health-disabled (ŤZP) holders collecting payments personally
questions.explanation: ŤZP (ťažko zdravotne postihnutý) holders are exempt — but only while they personally handle payments.

questions.question: What happens to the ŤZP exemption if a non-disabled employee starts handling payments?
options: It must be reviewed every 5 years
questions.explanation: The exemption is lost the moment a non-disabled person handles payments on their behalf.

questions.question: What does SORP stand for?
questions.explanation: SORP = software-based online cash register, added in Act 384/2025.

questions.question: Which register type is a free web/mobile app provided by the Financial Administration itself?
questions.explanation: VRP (currently VRP2) is government-built and free to use.

questions.statement: VRP must be certified by FRSR before it can be used.
questions.explanation: VRP requires no certification because the government built it itself. ORP and SORP must be certified.

questions.statement: ORP and SORP devices must be certified by FRSR before they can be sold.
questions.explanation: Certification is mandatory for any commercial register product.

questions.question: On iOS, which payment terminal does VRP2 support?
questions.explanation: On iOS, only SumUp is supported. Android users get both SumUp and GP Tom.

questions.question: GP Tom is best described as…
options: A portable card reader by SumUp
Global Payments' SoftPOS that turns an Android phone into a contactless card reader
questions.explanation: GP Tom is Global Payments' SoftPOS solution for Android phones.

questions.question: What format is used for eKasa data messages?
questions.explanation: XML messages over HTTPS, signed with the seller's certificate.

questions.question: If eKasa returns error -1 ('system busy') or a section 2.6 error, what is the merchant allowed to do?
options: Continue operating; the message is queued in CHDÚ for retry
Email FRSR for permission to continue
questions.explanation: These specific errors allow continued operation; other errors halt new transactions.

questions.statement: A certified register may print and hand over a receipt before eKasa confirms registration.
questions.explanation: Hard rule — receipts cannot be issued until eKasa confirms registration.

questions.statement: Rejected transactions still increment the receipt sequence number.
questions.explanation: Rejected transactions do NOT increment the sequence number.

questions.question: What does CHDÚ stand for?
questions.explanation: Chránené dátové úložisko — Protected Data Storage.

questions.question: The CHDÚ follows the WORM principle. WORM means…
questions.explanation: Write Once Read Many — same principle as CD-R. Once written, data cannot be modified or deleted by anyone.

questions.question: Software CHDÚ uses chained hashes built with which algorithm?
questions.explanation: Each file's hash includes the previous file's hash, forming an SHA-512 chain.

questions.question: What is the minimum required CHDÚ capacity?
questions.explanation: Minimum 8 GB; recommended capacity covers ~3 years / 300,000 receipts.

questions.question: How is data stored in CHDÚ?
questions.explanation: By law, so inspectors can read it directly without keys or tools.

questions.statement: If CHDÚ is full or inaccessible, the device can still issue receipts as long as eKasa is online.
questions.explanation: No CHDÚ = no receipts. The signing certificate must be fetched from CHDÚ for every signed message.

questions.question: How many ACTIVE credential sets can a register have at any one time?
questions.explanation: Maximum 2 active credential sets, though CHDÚ can store up to 20 historic sets.

questions.question: How many days BEFORE expiry can new credentials be generated?
questions.explanation: There is a 30-day pre-expiry window.

questions.question: Where are eKasa credentials generated?
options: In the FRSR Personal Internet Zone (financnasprava.sk)
questions.explanation: Credentials are generated through the Personal Internet Zone.

questions.statement: Lost VRP credentials can be recovered using a simple password reset.
questions.explanation: There is no password reset. The merchant must file a 'Notice of Lost, Misused, or Stolen' form, the register goes READ ONLY, and a new register code is issued.

questions.question: Which item-type code represents a regular positive sale?
questions.explanation: K = kladná (positive) — regular sales, advances, returnable packaging.

questions.question: How are returns/refunds processed in eKasa?
options: Via a dedicated 'Return of Merchandise' receipt referencing the original document ID
By issuing a negative invoice on paper
questions.explanation: A dedicated Return-of-Merchandise document creates an audit trail by referencing the original receipt.

questions.question: Which consumer mobile app lets shoppers scan an eKasa receipt's QR code to verify it?
questions.explanation: The eKasa peňaženka — 'eKasa eWallet' — is the official consumer verification app.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT one of the two automatic merchant reports in the eKasa portal?
questions.explanation: The two automatic reports are Summary Revenues and Recorded Documents. Inventory is not part of eKasa.

questions.question: Which hash algorithm is explicitly BANNED by the eKasa cryptographic requirements?
questions.explanation: SHA-1 is banned. SHA-2 and SHA-3 families are allowed.

questions.question: Minimum RSA key size accepted by the eKasa spec?
questions.explanation: RSA n ≥ 2048, per NIST SP 800-131A Rev 1.

questions.question: Which symmetric mode is explicitly forbidden?
questions.explanation: ECB is banned because it leaks patterns; any other mode of AES, 3-key TDEA, or ChaCha20 is acceptable.

questions.statement: On a certified Android-based eKasa register, USB/ADB debugging must be disabled and the bootloader locked.
questions.explanation: Plus: fastboot disabled, only signed apps installable, and SD/eMMC unreadable at recovery level.

questions.statement: Manufacturers may keep the OS image and device passwords confidential from FRSR.
questions.explanation: They must hand over a 1:1 OS binary image and all device passwords to FRSR.

questions.question: How must the tax inspector's USB drive be formatted?
questions.explanation: exFAT is required.

questions.question: What marker file must be present in the USB root to trigger the export?
questions.explanation: A file named exactly 'FSSR_export' (no extension) in the USB root.

questions.question: What is exported to the USB?
options: Only the latest day's receipts, encrypted
The entire CHDÚ contents as an unencrypted ZIP
Nothing — inspectors must use a separate tool
questions.explanation: Full CHDÚ contents as an unencrypted ZIP, named <register_code>YYMMDD-hhmmss.zip.

questions.statement: At every system start, the device recomputes the entire SHA-512 chain from the beginning and compares against stored .SYNC files.
questions.explanation: Any mismatch blocks all writes and reports the exact .SYNC file location of the failure.

questions.statement: After a CHDÚ integrity failure, the merchant can clear the error themselves and resume sales.
questions.explanation: The device cannot resume operations without manufacturer intervention.

questions.question: When did eKasa become mandatory for ALL existing businesses?
questions.explanation: July 1, 2019 — universal coverage achieved.

questions.question: What changed for eKasa users on January 1, 2025?
options: New VAT rates were introduced; all devices needed updates
questions.explanation: VRP got the update automatically; ORP users had to wait for manufacturer updates.

questions.question: Which spec version was published on December 8, 2025?
questions.explanation: v1.8 — added requirements for SORP and cloud-hosted CHDÚ.

questions.question: Which country runs the 'Lotteria degli Scontrini' — a lottery linked to receipts?
questions.explanation: Italy. Hungary and Poland have their own real-time fiscal register systems too.

questions.question: What is the maximum validity period of an eKasa device certification?
questions.explanation: Maximum 5 years. FRSR must decide on a certification application within 90 days.

questions.question: Manufacturers submit source code to FRSR. Why does FRSR insist on compiling it themselves?
options: Pre-built binaries are not accepted — FRSR must compile on their own machines to ensure trust
questions.explanation: FRSR refuses pre-built binaries to remove the chance of hidden modifications.

questions.question: Can the PPPEK and CHDÚ be certified independently of each other?
options: Yes, if from the same vendor
No — they must be certified together
questions.explanation: PPPEK and CHDÚ must be certified together.

questions.statement: eKasa stores the personal identity of every consumer who buys something.
questions.explanation: The system stores transaction data, not personal customer data. Customer ID is optional and used only for cases like B2B invoices.

questions.statement: A merchant can simply switch off the device to avoid eKasa, with no consequences.
questions.explanation: If the device is off, the merchant cannot legally accept payments. Inspectors can check whether sales are happening during business hours.

questions.statement: This marks the end of the eKasa presentation question set.
questions.explanation: Separator marker — the eKasa block runs from question 41 to 66.

title: Insane Facts From the Animal Kingdom

questions.question: The pistol shrimp snaps its claw so violently that it creates a cavitation bubble. What insane thing happens when that bubble collapses?
options: It produces a faint glow of light and reaches temperatures briefly comparable to the surface of the Sun
It freezes the water around it solid
It releases a small electric shock
questions.explanation: Sonoluminescence — the collapsing bubble briefly hits ~4,700 °C (close to the Sun's surface) and emits a flash of light. Also one of the loudest sounds in the ocean.

questions.question: Turritopsis dohrnii, a tiny jellyfish, is famous for being able to do what?
options: Revert to its juvenile polyp stage and effectively start its life over — making it biologically 'immortal'
Live entirely without water for years
questions.explanation: When stressed or injured, it transforms its cells back into the polyp stage and starts again — earning it the nickname 'the immortal jellyfish'.

questions.question: Tardigrades ('water bears') have famously survived which of the following?
options: Only the bottom of the ocean
The vacuum of outer space, including direct solar radiation
questions.explanation: In a 2007 ESA experiment, tardigrades survived 10 days exposed to the vacuum of space and cosmic radiation. They can also handle near-absolute-zero temperatures and pressures over 6x the deepest ocean trench.

questions.question: The mantis shrimp's punch is so fast that it…
options: Travels faster than a .22 caliber bullet
Boils water and produces a flash of light from cavitation, and accelerates faster than a bullet from a gun
questions.explanation: Its dactyl club accelerates at over 10,000 g, momentarily superheats the water via cavitation, and the collapsing bubble produces a flash of light — a second 'shockwave' strike even if the first misses.

questions.question: Solve for x:    3x + 7 = 22
Solve the system:    x + y = 7    and    2x − y = 8
questions.explanation: Add the two equations: (x + y) + (2x − y) = 7 + 8 → 3x = 15 → x = 5. Then y = 7 − 5 = 2.

questions.question: What is the derivative of    f(x) = 3x² + 2x − 5 ?
questions.explanation: Power rule: d/dx(3x²) = 6x, d/dx(2x) = 2, and the derivative of a constant (−5) is 0. So f'(x) = 6x + 2.
